name: Egg Custard Tarts
recipeInstructions:
Cream the butter and lard together. Add the egg and sugar, beating well. Gradually add the flour, 1 cup at a time, until the dough is crumbly. Gather into a ball with your hands, kneading lightly. You may chill while preparing the filling.
Egg Custard Filling: Ensure all ingredients are at room temperature. Beat whole eggs and yolks together on low speed; do not over-beat. Add sugar, then milk, then half-and-half. Let rest 10-15 minutes. Skim foam from mixture.
Shape dough into 24 balls and press each into a 2-1/2 inch tart shell, forming an even layer across the bottom and up the sides. Fill shells almost to the top with filling. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C). Place tarts on a baking sheet and bake for 45 minutes. Cool for 10-15 minutes. Loosen edges slightly with a toothpick; tart shell should come away easily.
Rhoda Yee's comments: Ensure filling ingredients are at room temperature and beaten over warm water to prevent separation during baking. Skimming foam yields a golden, creamy custard with a velvety texture. Avoid high heat to prevent bubbling and bursting.
